idea-plugin.name: Sigil - Coding Assistant
idea-plugin.description:
Sigil — a coding assistant plugin for IntelliJ IDEA that integrates AI-assisted development directly into the IDE. Powered by OpenCode.

Features

  • AI chat inside the IDE — Ask questions, paste code, attach files, and get answers in a side panel. Responses render markdown, code blocks, and inline tool usage.
  • Sessions — Keep conversations organized. Each session tracks its own token usage and estimated cost.
  • Context panel — See how much context you're using, what model is active, and a rough cost breakdown.
  • Code review — Request reviews from the OpenCode server and see results in a dedicated Review tab and as inline editor gutter comments. Resolve, reply to, or delete comments directly from the editor.
  • Slash commands — Type / in the input to trigger commands like /clear and /cancel, plus any commands exposed by the connected OpenCode server.
  • Tool permissions and MCP support — Connect to MCP servers and pick which tools the agent is allowed to call. Built-in support for registering IntelliJ's own bundled MCP server.

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 or newer.
  • The opencode CLI installed and available on your system PATH, or configured in the plugin settings (Settings → Tools → Sigil).

How it works

The plugin launches a local opencode serve process and communicates with it via HTTP REST and SSE on localhost. All network traffic stays on your machine — no data is sent to external servers by the plugin itself.

Review workflow

The review feature stores comments as JSON files under .review/ in your project. Comments track a line range, message, severity, and status. Because the comments live in the repo as regular files, they can be committed alongside the code — a branch can carry its own review state.

  • /review — asks OpenCode to review the current change and write review comments.
  • /review-perform — runs a stricter, adversarial review.
  • /review-resolve — sends open comments to the agent and asks it to fix the code.
  • /review-recheck — re-runs the review with awareness of existing comments and replies.

UI

Built with Jewel, JetBrains' Compose-based UI toolkit for IntelliJ plugins, so it looks at home in Darcula, New UI, or any custom theme.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0

  • Initial public release.
  • AI chat with SSE streaming, markdown rendering, and inline tool usage display.
  • Session management with token usage and cost tracking.
  • Context panel showing model, token breakdown, and cost.
  • Code review with inline editor gutter comments and Review tab.
  • Slash command palette with local and server commands.
  • MCP server integration with tool permissions.
  • File and image attachments.
  • Configurable server port, log level, and binary path.

action.description: Add a review comment on the selected line range
